response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and systems developer with solid TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture experience, but the core requirement for this role — deep C++ expertise — is nearly absent from his profile. His only documented C++ work is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not appear in his skills section. His recent career trajectory has moved firmly toward web and TypeScript stacks. Beyond the language gap, there is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL), familiarity with industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or people management experience, all of which are central to this Senior Manager role.
strengths: National Instruments background provides genuine industrial software context (C/C++ components for measurement engineering tooling)
Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems thinking across multiple roles, including a self-built geo-data platform
Proficient in React and Node.js, which are listed as advantageous skills for the position
concerns: C++ is not a current or primary skill — last meaningful C++ work was over a decade ago; entirely absent from his skills list
Zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity expertise: no IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or related industrial protocol experience
No demonstrated leadership or management track record despite the role being a Senior Manager position with mentorship responsibilities
